habitat: Subtropical/tropical moist montane forests; rocky streams and gullies in mountains
story: An icon of the skies, the Bornean Whistling Thrush (Myophonus borneensis) is a species from the Muscicapidae family. It thrives in Subtropical/tropical moist montane forests; rocky streams and gullies in mountains, where it interacts with its environment in unique ways. With a conservation status of Least Concern (LC), the Bornean Whistling Thrush is a focus for conservationists worldwide. Understanding the needs of the Bornean Whistling Thrush helps us better comprehend the challenges facing our planet's wildlife. This poster is a celebration of the Bornean Whistling Thrush, a bird that inspires wonder and highlights the critical importance of conservation efforts.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
